creator: @quebert
executive_summary: Trump formally announced a US Navy blockade of the Strait of Hormuz Sunday April 12 after US-Iran Islamabad talks collapsed (Vance walked out after 21 hours) — sending WTI crude +9% to $105.28. This full-universe 55-ticker run unveils three extraordinary new SaaS oversold setups: NOW (RSI=17.1, fwdPE=16.5 — the cheapest it has ever traded vs fundamentals), SNOW (RSI=16.8), and CRM (RSI=22.2). NOW is the Pick of the Run: at $83 it offers a 4.5:1 risk/reward with 10 days to a natural catalyst earnings (April 22). Real F&G=16 (Extreme Fear). BTC ETF inflows $787M/week contradict panic sentiment. All overbought tickers (13 excluded by RSI≥70 gate) and negative-revenue stocks (TSLA, XOM) filtered out. 8 picks total: 5 direct buys + 3 DCA positions.

macro_environment.summary: Hormuz blockade formally announced April 12 — new escalation beyond Saturday's failed talks. WTI crude +9% to $105.28. Gold -2% on rate cut delay fears. BTC -3% risk-off. F&G=16 (Extreme Fear) — historically the strongest contrarian buy zone. Q1 earnings season opens this week: JPM/GS/WFC/C (Apr 14), BAC/MS (Apr 15), NFLX/TSM/ASML (Apr 16). Enterprise SaaS sector in mass capitulation — NOW/SNOW/CRM at historically extreme oversold levels.

cross_sector_insights.insight: NOW (RSI=17.1, fwdPE=16.5) is the most extraordinary setup in the 55-ticker universe
cross_sector_insights.implication: Extending the watchlist to 55 tickers reveals ServiceNow at fwdPE=16.5 — cheaper than MSFT — with 20.7% revenue growth and 37.3% FCF margin. Earnings Apr 22 in 10 days provide the natural re-rating catalyst. Dan Ives (Wedbush) explicitly called the SaaS sell-off 'overdone.' 4.5:1 risk/reward makes it the top new pick of this run.

cross_sector_insights.insight: Three SaaS companies at RSI < 23 simultaneously — historically rare AI disruption panic bottom
cross_sector_insights.implication: NOW(17.1), SNOW(16.8), CRM(22.2) are all in extreme oversold territory simultaneously. Goldman's software sector downgrade + AI disruption fear has created a mass capitulation event. RSI < 20 readings in quality SaaS historically produce 30-60% bounces over 3-6 months. NOW is Buy direct; CRM is DCA (data breach risk); SNOW is Wait (expensive fwdPE=49.9).

cross_sector_insights.insight: Geopolitical bifurcation: oil +9%, gold -2% — diverging signals for different inflation scenarios
cross_sector_insights.implication: Hormuz blockade is inflationary (oil spike) BUT also delays Fed rate cuts (gold headwind near-term). CVX benefits directly. XAU needs DCA patience. CL (crude oil ETF) is NOT a moderate-profile trade — binary resolution risk with Trump stating 'Hormuz open fairly soon'.

cross_sector_insights.insight: Healthcare deep value uncovered: GILD fwdPE=11.1 for 19.4% growth is mispriced
cross_sector_insights.implication: Full 55-ticker run reveals Gilead as the most attractively valued healthcare name. Earnings April 23 (11 days) with 4-beat streak and lenacapavir HIV prevention expansion is a clean catalyst setup. Healthcare sector -10% Q1 headwind is managed-care specific (UNH), not biotech.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Microsoft Corporation
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=39.5 near SMA200 support ($356) — textbook contrarian re-entry for the world's most dominant enterprise AI platform. fwdPE=19.7 is historically the cheapest MSFT has traded in years. Azure cloud 25%+ growth confirmed. 'Copilot Code Red' shows Nadella forcing AI monetization urgency. Earnings April 29 with 4-beat streak (+8% avg surprise) — clean 17-day runway. Dan Ives: 'MSFT sell-off is overdone.' $53.6B FCF, 68.6% gross margin.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: ServiceNow
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=17.1 — MOST EXTREME oversold reading across all 55 tickers. At $83, fwdPE=16.5 is CHEAPER THAN MSFT for a platform with +20.7% revenue YoY and 37.3% FCF margin ($4.95B FCF). UBS downgrade fully priced into -60% crash from $208.94 52w-high. Dan Ives: 'ServiceNow sell-off is overdone.' Earnings April 22 in 10 days with 4-beat streak — natural catalyst. Agentic AI platform uniquely positioned vs LLM disruptors. 4.5:1 risk/reward is the BEST in this portfolio.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Gilead Sciences
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: fwdPE=11.1 — extraordinary deep value; cheapest healthcare on forward earnings for its growth (+19.4% revenue, 82.5% gross margin, 32.6% FCF margin = $13.2B FCF). Trading above BOTH SMA50 and SMA200 — bullish technical structure unlike peers. Earnings April 23 (11 days) with 4-beat streak. Lenacapavir HIV prevention TAM expansion is multi-billion inflection point. Defensive non-correlated to oil/Iran.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Gold Spot
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Structural bull case accelerating: central banks buying record pace (China +5T, Turkey +118T March), BRICS+ de-dollarization, Gabelli $6,000+/oz and Goldman $5,800 targets. DCA not direct because Hormuz blockade creates near-term rate cut delay → gold headwind. Gold -17% from $5,608 52w-high creates attractive accumulation zone. CPI +3.3% structural. Thesis fully intact despite today's -2% dip.

Earnings May 20 provides 38-day runway. Sized at 70% given RSI proximity to gate.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Chevron Corporation
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=34.9 'excellent-oversold' entry ABOVE SMA200 support ($152.42). fwdPE=14.4 based on STALE oil assumptions — WTI at $105.28 creates MASSIVE earnings upside vs embedded estimates. Hormuz blockade keeps oil premium elevated. EV/EBITDA=8.5x sector discount. Earnings May 2 — consensus will be upgraded with $100+ oil. Chevron $10.7B FCF + Buffett-quality dividend.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Bitcoin
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: F&G=16 (Extreme Fear) historically strongest contrarian zone. BUT Hormuz blockade = active risk-off — DCA not single-entry. BTC ETF inflows $787M/week; IBIT $57.29B AUM; Coinbase Premium flipped positive = institutional accumulation in real-time. Strategy holds 766,970 BTC. BTC outperforming gold/equities/bonds since Iran conflict — digital gold narrative validating. BTC dominance 56.9% = safest crypto in risk-off.

risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=22.2 extreme oversold, fwdPE=22.0 reasonable, $10B FCF, 77.4% gross margin. DCA not direct: (1) Data breach quantification still pending; (2) 4,000 AI-driven job cuts = execution risk; (3) 9% revenue growth slowest in list; (4) No catalyst until ~May 28. Dan Ives calls sell-off overdone. Long-term thesis: Agentforce AI pivot.

watchlist.note: RSI=16.8 extreme oversold but fwdPE=49.9 elevated even post -56% crash. No near-term earnings (~May-June). Negative EV/EBITDA. Wait for $95-108 zone before DCA entry.
